No further find-and-replace cleanup is needed.

after.task: Investigate lane summary drift between handoff/status and workflow events
after.nextAction: Approved. All 105 tests pass. The event-recovery logic in resolveCurrentStateFromWorkflowEvents is correct: it only activates when the event snapshot date is strictly newer than the handoff file date, so legitimately newer handoff edits always win. readCurrentState now funnels both lane and single-handoff modes through the same event-aware path. Lane-mode status freshness derives from the most recently updated lane via the decorated lane states. The cumulative diff is large because it includes prior approved slices (reason codes, repair-needed, override flow, lane ids fix, doctor --repair, history compaction) — all of which were individually reviewed and approved. The new stale-branch regression test validates the specific failure mode where a tracked handoff file falls behind untracked .btrain/events after branch movement.

after.task: WS5: Visual verification and integration testing of lane-bound agentchattr workspaces

after.task: WS5: Visual verification and integration testing of lane-bound agentchattr workspaces
after.nextAction: Approved. 428 insertions across 5 files implementing the full WS5 lane workspace surface. Key findings: (1) btrain poller correctly resolves binary via PATH + npm global + node fallback, resolves repo_path relative to agentchattr dir, caches with threading lock, broadcasts diffs over WebSocket. (2) Good addition: btrain lane state sent on WebSocket connect so fresh loads get instant data instead of waiting 15s. (3) Lane REST endpoints (/api/btrain/lanes, /api/btrain/lanes/{id}, /api/btrain/lane_archives/{id}) return correct cached data. (4) Auto-archive on task change correctly exports lane messages to zip, clears channel, inserts system divider. (5) Lane pill tabs match dashboard indicator-pill style with status animations (hop/chug/squish) and staggered delays. (6) Lane header matches dashboard lane-card format: LANE A + [STATUS] badge, W//R// agent meta, task desc, locks tag, collapsible details. (7) --json flag on btrain status outputs valid structured JSON with all decorated lane fields. (8) 109 tests pass, zero console errors, visually verified at mobile and desktop widths.

details.summary: Approved. 428 insertions across 5 files implementing the full WS5 lane workspace surface. Key findings: (1) btrain poller correctly resolves binary via PATH + npm global + node fallback, resolves repo_path relative to agentchattr dir, caches with threading lock, broadcasts diffs over WebSocket. (2) Good addition: btrain lane state sent on WebSocket connect so fresh loads get instant data instead of waiting 15s. (3) Lane REST endpoints (/api/btrain/lanes, /api/btrain/lanes/{id}, /api/btrain/lane_archives/{id}) return correct cached data. (4) Auto-archive on task change correctly exports lane messages to zip, clears channel, inserts system divider. (5) Lane pill tabs match dashboard indicator-pill style with status animations (hop/chug/squish) and staggered delays. (6) Lane header matches dashboard lane-card format: LANE A + [STATUS] badge, W//R// agent meta, task desc, locks tag, collapsible details. (7) --json flag on btrain status outputs valid structured JSON with all decorated lane fields. (8) 109 tests pass, zero console errors, visually verified at mobile and desktop widths.

details.summary: Review found implementation mismatches in docs/architecture.md. The lane-context section omits repair-needed and shows fields/format that wrapper.py does not inject. The security section describes a fixed 127.0.0.1:8300 origin requirement and misses Bearer access on /api/rules/*, which does not match app.py. Please update the doc to match the current code paths, then re-handoff for review.

after.task: Architecture documentation: btrain + agentchattr system diagrams and docs
after.nextAction: Address GPT's review findings in the same lane and re-handoff for review.

details.summary: The enforcement section overstates two protections: it says the pre-handoff skill blocks invalid needs-review transitions, but the hard check actually lives in btrain handoff update; and it says pre-commit blocks commits on files locked by other lanes, but the installed hook only blocks non-handoff commits while any handoff is in needs-review. Please correct docs/architecture.md so the enforcement layers match the implementation.
